name: Lookup Patterns
description: Questions on dict lookups, get, and in.

questions.question: To get a value with a default if the key is missing, use:
questions.explanation: d.get(key, default) returns default when key is not in d; no KeyError.

questions.question: Checking if key k is in dict d is:
questions.explanation: Dict membership (k in d) is O(1) average due to hashing.

questions.question: defaultdict(int) gives missing keys a value of:
questions.explanation: defaultdict(int) uses int() as the factory; int() is 0.

questions.question: d.setdefault(k, v) does what?
choices: Returns d[k] if k in d, else sets d[k]=v and returns v
questions.explanation: setdefault returns d[k] if present; otherwise sets d[k]=v and returns v.

questions.question: Iterating over a dict with for k in d: iterates over:
questions.explanation: for k in d iterates over the keys. Use d.values() or d.items() for the others.
